review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "conclusion": "用`Lt`表替换`Cased`表以节省空间，但逻辑存在错误。",
    "description": "在`char::is_cased`方法中，新的实现逻辑`unicode::Lowercase(self) || unicode::Uppercase(self) || unicode::Lt(self)`是错误的。根据Unicode定义，`Cased`属性包含所有大小写字母（Lowercase、Uppercase、Titlecase）。`Lt`（Letter, titlecase）只是Titlecase的一个子集，它仅包含那些作为单个代码点存在的标题大小写字母（如'ǅ'），而`Titlecase`属性还包括那些通过特殊映射（如`to_titlecase`）产生的字符。因此，新的逻辑会遗漏许多属于`Cased`但不属于`Lt`的字符，导致`is_cased`返回错误结果。",
    "evidence": "文件: library/core/src/char/methods.rs\n@@ -810,7 +810,7 @@ impl char {\n         match self {\n             'a'..='z' | 'A'..='Z' => true,\n             '\\0'..='\\u{A9}' => false,\n-            _ => unicode::Cased(self),\n+            _ => unicode::Lowercase(self) || unicode::Uppercase(self) || unicode::Lt(self),\n         }\n     }",
    "advice": "需要重新评估如何在不使用`Cased`表的情况下正确实现`is_cased`。一个可能的方案是：`is_cased = is_lowercase || is_uppercase || is_titlecase`，其中`is_titlecase`的实现应基于Unicode的`Titlecase`属性（可能通过`to_titlecase`映射或一个更完整的表），而不是仅使用`Lt`。或者，如果目标只是节省空间，可以考虑其他优化方式，而不是直接删除`Cased`表。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "conclusion": "测试用例引用错误，导致测试无法编译或测试错误功能。",
    "description": "在测试文件`unicode.rs`中，`lt`测试函数错误地引用了`unicode_data::cased::lookup`，但`cased`模块已被删除。这会导致编译错误。同时，测试函数名称为`lt`，但测试的却是`char::is_titlecase`，这可能是正确的，但函数名和测试数据（`test_data::LT`）与调用的函数不匹配，容易引起混淆。",
    "evidence": "文件: library/coretests/tests/unicode.rs\n@@ -60,9 +60,9 @@ fn case_ignorable() {\n \n #[test]\n #[cfg_attr(miri, ignore)] // Miri is too slow\n-fn cased() {\n-    test_boolean_property(test_data::CASED, unicode_data::cased::lookup);\n-    test_boolean_property(test_data::CASED, char::is_cased);\n+fn lt() {\n+    test_boolean_property(test_data::LT, unicode_data::cased::lookup);\n+    test_boolean_property(test_data::LT, char::is_titlecase);\n }",
    "advice": "1. 将`unicode_data::cased::lookup`的引用更正为`unicode_data::lt::lookup`。\n2. 考虑是否需要为`is_cased`添加新的测试，因为现有的`cased`测试已被替换。",
    "confidence": 1.0,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "conclusion": "`char::is_titlecase`的实现从逻辑推导改为直接查表，更简洁高效。",
    "description": "`is_titlecase`方法的实现从`self.is_cased() && !self.is_lowercase() && !self.is_uppercase()`改为直接调用`unicode::Lt(self)`。这是一个积极的改进，因为它直接使用了Unicode的`Lt`（Letter, titlecase）属性，逻辑更清晰，性能可能更好（直接查表 vs 三次函数调用和逻辑运算）。",
    "evidence": "文件: library/core/src/char/methods.rs\n@@ -919,7 +919,7 @@ impl char {\n     pub fn is_titlecase(self) -> bool {\n         match self {\n             '\\0'..='\\u{01C4}' => false,\n-            _ => self.is_cased() && !self.is_lowercase() && !self.is_uppercase(),\n+            _ => unicode::Lt(self),\n         }\n     }",
    "advice": "此变更设计良好，无需修改。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

commits:
[{'sha': '278536bbcb019d99fd239ec1942783544f9f17b2', 'message': 'Remove the `_description_fns` module.

`rustc_queries` generates a macro and two modules. One of the modules
looks like this:
```
mod _description_fns {
    ...

    #[allow(unused_variables)]
    pub fn hir_module_items<'tcx>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, key: LocalModDefId) -> String {
	format!("getting HIR module items in `{}`", tcx.def_path_str(key))
    }

    ...
}
```
Members of this module are then used in `TaggedQueryKey::description`.

This commit removes the `_description_fns` module entirely. For each
query we now instead generate a description closure that is used
instead. This closure is passed in the modifiers list.

This change simplifies `rustc_queries` quite a bit. It requires adding
another query modifier, but query modifiers are how other query-specific
details are already passed to the declarative macros, so it's more
consistent.'}, {'sha': '56d9ea6e8659b7a89ecc882d602a1b07585b00f2', 'message': "Add a `handle_cycle_error` query modifier.

This modifier indicates that a query has a custom handler for cycles.
That custom handler must be found at
`rustc_query_impl::handle_cycle_error::$name`.

This eliminates the need for `specialize_query_vtables`, which is the
current hack to install custom handlers. It's more lines of code in
total, but indicating special treatment of a query via a modifier in
`queries.rs` is more consistent with how other aspects of queries are
handled."}, {'sha': '63e44caeceff9cff9180ba58d2eaa109f2402ba9', 'message': 'Use typedefs for key and value types in custom cycle handlers.

This avoids having to copy the type from the query definition.'}]

commits:
[{'sha': '278536bbcb019d99fd239ec1942783544f9f17b2', 'message': 'Remove the `_description_fns` module.

`rustc_queries` generates a macro and two modules. One of the modules
looks like this:
```
mod _description_fns {
    ...

    #[allow(unused_variables)]
    pub fn hir_module_items<'tcx>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, key: LocalModDefId) -> String {
	format!("getting HIR module items in `{}`", tcx.def_path_str(key))
    }

    ...
}
```
Members of this module are then used in `TaggedQueryKey::description`.

This commit removes the `_description_fns` module entirely. For each
query we now instead generate a description closure that is used
instead. This closure is passed in the modifiers list.

This change simplifies `rustc_queries` quite a bit. It requires adding
another query modifier, but query modifiers are how other query-specific
details are already passed to the declarative macros, so it's more
consistent.'}, {'sha': '56d9ea6e8659b7a89ecc882d602a1b07585b00f2', 'message': "Add a `handle_cycle_error` query modifier.

This modifier indicates that a query has a custom handler for cycles.
That custom handler must be found at
`rustc_query_impl::handle_cycle_error::$name`.

This eliminates the need for `specialize_query_vtables`, which is the
current hack to install custom handlers. It's more lines of code in
total, but indicating special treatment of a query via a modifier in
`queries.rs` is more consistent with how other aspects of queries are
handled."}, {'sha': '63e44caeceff9cff9180ba58d2eaa109f2402ba9', 'message': 'Use typedefs for key and value types in custom cycle handlers.

This avoids having to copy the type from the query definition.'}]
